title: crash_info: potential false positive on first power-on boot
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/crash_info.c
Lines: 52-70 (crash_info_check_and_clear function)

On the very first power-on boot (not warm reset), the __noinit crash_info structure contains uninitialized garbage values. There is a 1-in-2^32 probability that this garbage could match CRASH_MAGIC (0xDEAD0001), causing a false 'previous crash' log message with garbage data.

Current behavior:
1. First boot: __noinit RAM = garbage
2. crash_info_check_and_clear() checks if crash_info.magic == CRASH_MAGIC
3. If garbage matches (1 in ~4 billion), logs a false crash warning with garbage reason/location/extra

While the probability is extremely low, it could cause confusion during initial deployment debugging.

Potential fix options:
1. Accept the 1-in-2^32 risk (current behavior, probably acceptable)
2. Add a secondary magic/checksum over the crash_info fields
3. Use a pattern like 0xDEADxxxx where xxxx is a CRC of the other fields

Severity: P4 (Low) - The probability is negligible for most deployments, but documenting this edge case is valuable.
close_reason: Implemented dual-magic validation for crash_info to prevent false positive on first power-on. Updated lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/crash_info.c. Tests/build verified via cmake test. Pushed commit 91d54c589.
